name: SOGO Kids Pack
white: A suspicious goose.
Three raccoons operating a lawnmower.
A booger that whispers your name.
Grandpa's secret ninja past.
A guinea pig with a dark secret.
Explaining to Grandma why the smoke alarm is going off again.
The school nurse's jar of teeth.
Double-bouncing your little brother into the neighbor's yard.
Licking the frozen flagpole anyway.
Mashed potatoes sculpted into a screaming face.
A velociraptor on a scooter.
Wizard dandruff.
The mail carrier's ancient grudge.
A pigeon with a business plan.
A cannonball into the kiddie pool.
The forbidden meat at the back of the fridge.
Cereal milk that has seen things.
A worm named Greg.
The dog's court-ordered obedience school.
Farting in the elevator and blaming the baby.
A fart that sets off car alarms.
A clogged toilet at someone else's house.
The plunger of destiny.
Forty pounds of spaghetti.
Eating soup with a fork out of spite.
Ketchup on absolutely everything.
A hot dog that is 90% mystery.
Deep-frying the birthday cake.
The last chicken nugget, which is legally mine.
A water gun filled with gravy.
A cheese so stinky it has its own weather system.
My imaginary friend's imaginary friend.
An evil twin who is worse at math.
Mom's minivan doing sick jumps.
Grandma's driving.
Aunt Deb's potato salad.
My cousin, who eats crayons.
A cat plotting something in the shadows.
A squirrel with a sword.
Ninety-nine ducks in the living room.
A moose at the drive-thru window.
A skunk at the sleepover.
A llama that spits with deadly accuracy.
The class hamster's daring escape.
Show-and-tell gone horribly wrong.
The teachers' lounge, where secrets live.
A field trip to the mattress store.
The gym teacher's toupee, airborne.
A tuba full of oatmeal.
Twenty-five recorders playing "Hot Cross Buns."
Karate-chopping a wedding cake.
Riding a shopping cart down Dead Man's Hill.
A knight who is afraid of chickens.
A wizard who knows one spell, and it's for soup.
A pirate with excellent dental hygiene.
A ghost that just wants to fold your laundry.
Zeus, but as a substitute teacher.
A mermaid who can't swim.
Bigfoot's embarrassing yearbook photo.
The Loch Ness Monster's weird little cousin.
A dragon that hoards expired coupons.
A unicorn with road rage.
A vampire who is afraid of the dark.
A zombie doing gentle yoga.
A werewolf with a lint roller.
A T. rex trying to make its bed.
A robot that runs on apple juice.
An alien abducting the wrong cow.
Emergency backup underpants.
A cape made of bath towels.
The itchy holiday sweater.
Glitter that never, ever comes off.
A waterslide made of pudding.
A belly flop from the high dive.
That one warm spot in the pool.
A sandcastle with a working toilet.
Seagulls with zero respect.
A crab in your swim trunks.
Sunscreen directly in the eyes.
The five-second rule, but for gum.
A burp that answers the teacher's question.
Armpit noises during the moment of silence.
A museum of belly button lint.
A puddle of warm garbage juice.
Earwax candles.
Kissing the dog on the mouth.
The Tooth Fairy's black market.
Santa's legal team.
The Easter Bunny's day job.
Elves on strike.
A snowman that knows too much.
Snow of a suspicious color.
Hot chocolate with forty marshmallows.
A gingerbread man screaming in the oven.
The wishbone breaking in my favor.
The annual turkey-fryer fireball.
A piñata that fights back.
A birthday clown with unfinished business.
A bounce house rolling away with kids still inside.
Blowing out somebody else's birthday candles.
A balloon animal that took four hours.
The claw machine that owes me money.
A knockoff video-game plumber.
Flipping the table on family game night.
Rolling a one at the worst possible moment.
Getting destroyed at checkers by Grandpa.
The dice rolling under the refrigerator forever.
Rules Dad made up on the spot.
Holding the last puzzle piece hostage.
A tractor with no brakes.
A cookie tin full of sewing supplies.
The lawnmower achieving consciousness.
A leaf-blower duel at dawn.
Falling into the toilet at 3 a.m.
A hand dryer powerful enough to remove skin.
A porta-potty tipping over.
Coming in last at the pie-eating contest.
Competitive eating, but it's salad.
Sleeping inside a giant calzone.
Replacing my blood with nacho cheese.
A warm pickle smoothie.
The ice cream truck song at midnight.
A vending machine that only dispenses bees.
A casserole with a heartbeat.
Cookies that turn out to be raisin.
A burrito that is molten outside and frozen in the middle.
The TV remote, missing since March.
A couch-cushion fort with a strict no-parents policy.
Stepping barefoot on a tiny plastic brick.
The Wi-Fi going out for four minutes.
Watching videos at full volume in a quiet waiting room.
Grandma texting in all caps.
A goat on the school roof.
A human-sized hamster ball.
Taking the cat for a walk against its will.
A viking funeral for the class goldfish.
A parrot that tells Mom everything.
Ants with a plan.
The spider that lives in the mailbox.
A snail with somewhere to be.
Finding a slug in your shoe.
Sock puppets reenacting Mom and Dad's argument.
A hiccup attack during the spelling bee.
Milk coming out of your nose in front of everyone.
A library book that is fourteen years overdue.
A car trip with zero bathroom stops.
Asking "Are we there yet?" forty times.
Losing my funnel cake on the teacup ride.
A ferris wheel stuck at the top.
The roller coaster photo where my soul leaves my body.
A fast-food mascot with dead eyes.
A backpack that weighs more than me.
A cupcake topped with pencil shavings.
Sniffing the good markers.
Giving a book report on a book I did not open.
A baking-soda volcano the size of a real volcano.
Cursive.
Dad's air-guitar solo at a red light.
Grandpa's forty-five-minute story with no ending.
Purse candy from 1987.
A couch sealed in plastic.
Drinking from the garden hose like an animal.
Launching off the swing at maximum height.
Hopping off the seesaw without warning.
Hiding so well that everyone stops looking.
The floor being actual lava.
The neighbor kid who bites.
A full-contact game of musical chairs.
The monster under my bed, who pays no rent.
A night-light visible from space.
Sleepwalking into the neighbors' kitchen.
Whatever happens to the first kid who falls asleep at the sleepover.
Cocooning into my blanket and emerging as a moth.
Tying a loose tooth to a drone and pressing "go."
Answering the dentist's questions with a mouth full of fingers.
A wart shaped like the principal.
Holding in a fart for the entire school assembly.
Flicking a booger onto the ceiling fan.
Camping in a tent that is 10% waterproof.
Mosquitoes that know my name.
Poison ivy in a place I cannot mention.
A bear politely going through our cooler.
Catching an old boot and mounting it on the wall.
Quicksand, which was supposed to be a bigger problem.
A skeleton washing the dishes.
A mummy running out of toilet paper.
A wishing well that wishes back.
The royal food taster calling in sick.
A catapult loaded with meatballs.
The kraken, here for the pool party.
Medusa getting a haircut.
Autocorrect changing every word to "toot."
A voice assistant that refuses to stop playing polka.
Scoring the winning goal for the other team.
A trophy for eleventh place.
The junk drawer, which contains everything and nothing.
Finding my toys at our own garage sale.
An avalanche of food containers with no matching lids.
Mom's secret snack drawer.
Eating dessert first, like an outlaw.
